name: Kochi Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the rising sun over Tosa Bay and the spirited character of Kochi's people
purity, peace and the bright future of the prefecture

description: A deep-red field charged at the centre with a white emblem stylising the kanji 高 of the prefecture's name within a rounded katakana コ.
history: Kochi's prefectural symbol was adopted on 1 March 1953. The flag, with the white emblem on a maroon field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same period.

features.symbolism: a deep crimson red, evoking the rising sun over the Pacific and the warm climate of southern Shikoku

features.role: Kochi prefectural emblem (stylised katakana コ surrounding 千 / kanji 高)
features.symbolism: a stylised katakana コ (ko) enclosing an inner form, together representing the kanji 高 (ko, 'high') of 高知 (Kochi); the rounded outer form also evokes harmony and the dawning sun over Kochi.
